findings.summary: Multiple modules instantiate singletons at import time, creating implicit side effects on module load
evidence: health_check.py:285: `_checker = SystemHealthChecker()` runs at import time; SystemHealthChecker.__init__ sets start_time and allocates self.checks dict eagerly
security.py:394-397: four singleton objects instantiated at module level
sanitization.py:198: `_input_sanitizer = InputSanitizer()` instantiated at module level with dangerous_patterns list compiled on load
findings.suggestion: Wrap each singleton behind its getter function using lazy initialization (initialize on first call rather than at module import), removing import-time side effects

findings.summary: SecurityError in llm/core/sanitization.py is a bare Exception subclass disconnected from the TemplateError hierarchy
evidence: sanitization.py:191-194: `class SecurityError(Exception): pass` — does not inherit from TemplateError or any subclass
exceptions.py defines LLMError(TemplateError) which is the correct parent for LLM-layer errors
Callers that catch TemplateError broadly will miss SecurityError raises from the sanitizer
findings.suggestion: Change `class SecurityError(Exception)` to `class SecurityError(LLMError)` and import LLMError from infrastructure.core.exceptions, or add SecurityError to the exceptions module

findings.summary: infrastructure/reporting/__init__.py omits test_summary_generator and suite_summary_generator from its public API despite them being substantive modules in the package
evidence: __init__.py __all__ list has 17 names but excludes generate_summary_report, generate_markdown_report from test_summary_generator
suite_summary_generator.py exists in the package but is not imported or re-exported from __init__
findings.suggestion: Either add the missing generators to __init__.py exports or document them as internal-only with a module-level comment; inconsistency suggests the boundary is not intentional

findings.summary: health_check.py uses legacy typing.Dict/List/Optional while performance.py mixes legacy and builtin generics in the same file
evidence: health_check.py: `from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional` then uses Dict[str,Any] and List[str] throughout
performance.py: imports Dict, List, Optional, Union but also uses lowercase dict[str,Any] and list[dict[str,Any]] in method signatures in the same file
Both files have `from __future__ import annotations` which makes builtin generics safe, so the legacy forms are unnecessary
findings.suggestion: Standardize on lowercase builtin generics (dict, list, tuple, optional via X|None) throughout both files; the from __future__ import annotations already enables this

findings.summary: test_summary_generator.py defines a TypedDict class between two import blocks, violating PEP 8 import ordering
evidence: Lines 15-28: InfraResults TypedDict class is defined after stdlib imports but before infrastructure imports on lines 29-30
Standard ordering is: all imports first, then module-level definitions
findings.suggestion: Move the InfraResults TypedDict definition to after all imports (after line 32), or move lines 29-30 infrastructure imports to join the other imports at the top

findings.summary: SecurityError raised throughout sanitization.py is a bare Exception subclass, breaking the project-wide TemplateError catch-all contract
evidence: sanitization.py raises SecurityError in 8 locations for prompt injection, path traversal, and filename validation
SecurityError(Exception) defined at sanitization.py:191 — not connected to TemplateError
Code that does `except TemplateError` to catch all infrastructure errors will miss these security violations
findings.suggestion: Inherit SecurityError from LLMError (which inherits from TemplateError) so it participates in the standard exception hierarchy; add it to exceptions.py if it needs to be catchable across modules

findings.summary: infrastructure/scientific/templates.py raises bare TypeError and ValueError inside template string code rather than using TemplateError subclasses
evidence: templates.py:68: `raise TypeError(...)` inside template string for generated module code
templates.py:77: `raise ValueError(...)` inside template string for generated module code
templates.py:98,101: two more bare ValueError raises

These are in string literals (template content for new project modules), but the file-level functions at lines 68,77 are actual infrastructure code not template strings — they validate the template generation call itself
findings.suggestion: Distinguish clearly between template string content (acceptable to use builtins as examples) and actual infrastructure validation code; if the raises at lines 68-77 are in live infrastructure functions, replace with appropriate TemplateError subclasses

findings.summary: MissingDependencyError.__init__ performs OS detection and package manager discovery inside an exception constructor
evidence: Lines 303-333: imports platform and shutil, calls shutil.which('apt-get'), shutil.which('brew'), etc. inside __init__
OS detection and command generation is infrastructure logic, not exception domain
findings.suggestion: Extract install command generation to a standalone function in a utility module; exception constructors should only format message data

findings.summary: performance_context sets a dynamic attribute monitor.last_metrics not declared on PerformanceMonitor, bypassing the type system
evidence: Line 170: monitor.last_metrics = metrics  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
PerformanceMonitor has no last_metrics field; callers must know about this undocumented side effect
findings.suggestion: Add last_metrics: Optional[PerformanceMetrics] = None as a declared field on PerformanceMonitor

findings.summary: health_check.py instantiates SystemHealthChecker at module import time, triggering network and filesystem side effects on import
evidence: Line 285: _checker = SystemHealthChecker() runs at import time
SystemHealthChecker.__init__ records self.start_time = time.time() and the check methods probe DNS, /proc/uptime, psutil
findings.suggestion: Use lazy initialization: create _checker only inside get_health_status/quick_health_check, or make _checker = None and initialize on first call

findings.summary: platform and shutil are imported inside MissingDependencyError.__init__ on every instantiation rather than at module top-level
evidence: Lines 303 and 306: import platform and import shutil inside __init__ body
These are stdlib modules; importing inside a constructor is unusual and adds per-instantiation overhead
findings.suggestion: Move stdlib imports to top of exceptions.py module

findings.summary: Several tests in TestEnvironmentState and TestEnvironmentSetupIntegration test stdlib/OS behavior rather than infrastructure.core.environment functions
evidence: test_home_directory_accessible: calls Path.home() directly without using any environment module function
test_current_working_directory: calls Path.cwd() without exercising module
test_python_executable_exists: reads sys.executable directly
test_platform_info_available: checks sys.platform directly
test_version_components: checks sys.version_info attributes
findings.suggestion: Either remove these tests (they test Python stdlib, not the infrastructure module) or restructure them to call environment module functions and assert on their output

findings.summary: Two test files use MagicMock or patch despite the no-mocks policy
evidence: Grep confirmed MagicMock/patch present in these two files
findings.suggestion: Replace mock usage with real implementations: use pytest-httpserver for LLM HTTP calls (already used elsewhere), use real temp files for renderer tests

findings.summary: test_summary_generator.py and suite_summary_generator.py are near-identical ~420-line files with identical function bodies
evidence: Both files define identical functions: load_test_results, load_infrastructure_results, discover_active_projects, _calculate_weighted_coverage, _aggregate_counts, _is_ollama_available, generate_summary_report, generate_markdown_report, run_test_summary_generation
Line-for-line identical logic in both — copied-and-forgot pattern
findings.suggestion: Delete test_summary_generator.py entirely; all callers should import from suite_summary_generator.py directly

findings.summary: summary_generator.py has syntactically invalid Python — a logger assignment is injected mid-import block
evidence: Lines 8-19: `from infrastructure.reporting.suite_summary_generator import (` is followed by `from infrastructure.core.logging_utils import get_logger` and `logger = get_logger(__name__)` before the closing `)` of the first import
This is not valid Python and cannot be imported — a copy-paste artifact
findings.suggestion: Remove summary_generator.py entirely (it was meant to be a re-export shim); update any callers to import from suite_summary_generator directly

findings.summary: MissingDependencyError.__init__ runs platform detection and shutil.which calls to build recovery commands at exception-raise time
evidence: Lines 303-334: imports platform, calls platform.system().lower(), then calls shutil.which for apt-get/yum/dnf/pacman/brew inside __init__
This runs shell lookups every time the exception is constructed — over-engineered and fragile in CI environments
findings.suggestion: Move OS-specific recovery command generation to a standalone utility function called lazily, not inside __init__

findings.summary: Exception subclasses with pass bodies carry multi-line docstrings including Example: blocks with no behavioral content
evidence: NotADirectoryError: 10-line docstring on a class body of zero lines
InvalidFileFormatError: Example block on a `pass` body
MissingConfigurationError, CompilationError, PipelineError: single-sentence docstrings on pass-only classes that provide no insight beyond the name
findings.suggestion: Reduce exception subclass docstrings to a single descriptive line; remove Example blocks from classes with no custom logic

findings.summary: reporting/ contains 15 files with overlapping naming and unclear ownership boundaries between summary, reporter, generator, and parser roles
evidence: Three files named *_summary_generator.py with overlapping or duplicated responsibilities
coverage_parser.py and coverage_reporter.py both handle coverage data with no clear boundary documented
The __init__.py does not export symbols from coverage_parser, test_reporter, or suite_summary_generator — they live in the package but are invisible from outside
findings.suggestion: Consolidate: merge suite/test summary generators; rename coverage_parser into coverage_reporter or clearly document which is the internal parsing layer vs external API; reduce to ~10 well-named files

findings.summary: infrastructure/__init__.py re-exports 30+ symbols across 6 subpackages with silent optional-import fallbacks that leave None-valued names in the module namespace
evidence: Lines 25-75: six try/except ImportError blocks; on failure, symbols like generate_all_dashboards are set to None (type: ignore) and still appear in __all__
__all__ at line 77 lists 35 names including subpackage names ('core', 'validation', etc.) alongside concrete symbols — ambiguous surface
Two separate `warnings` imports: `import warnings as _warnings` at line 44 and `import warnings` at line 74
findings.suggestion: Remove the broad re-export surface from infrastructure/__init__.py; callers should import from subpackages directly; if a convenience layer is needed, limit it to the 4-5 most-used symbols with no None fallbacks in __all__

findings.summary: SecurityError in sanitization.py inherits from Exception rather than TemplateError, breaking the declared exception hierarchy
evidence: sanitization.py line 191: `class SecurityError(Exception): pass`
exceptions.py defines LLMError -> TemplateError as the base for all LLM-layer errors
SecurityError is raised by InputSanitizer which is called from LLMClient — it should be in the TemplateError hierarchy
findings.suggestion: Move SecurityError to infrastructure/core/exceptions.py as a subclass of LLMError (or TemplateError), remove it from sanitization.py

findings.summary: generate_multi_project_summary_report lives in pipeline_reporter.py but its concern belongs in multi_project_reporter.py
evidence: pipeline_reporter.py defines generate_multi_project_summary_report starting at line 45, importing MultiProjectResult from infrastructure.core.multi_project
multi_project_reporter.py exists as a dedicated module for this concern
The reporting __init__.py exports generate_multi_project_report from multi_project_reporter but pipeline_reporter also contains multi-project logic
findings.suggestion: Move generate_multi_project_summary_report out of pipeline_reporter.py into multi_project_reporter.py; pipeline_reporter.py should only handle single-project pipeline reports

findings.summary: health_check.py instantiates SystemHealthChecker() at module import time, creating a side-effectful singleton that starts a timer on import
evidence: Line 285: `_checker = SystemHealthChecker()` at module level
SystemHealthChecker.__init__ sets `self.start_time = time.time()` — timer starts at import time, not first use
The module-level checker is used by quick_health_check() and get_health_status() as pass-through wrappers with no added value
findings.suggestion: Use lazy initialization: instantiate _checker inside the module-level functions on first call, or use functools.lru_cache on a factory function

findings.summary: Three distinct 'stage result' dataclasses exist for the same pipeline-stage concept across three modules
evidence: checkpoint.py defines StageResult with fields: name, exit_code, duration, timestamp, completed, status
pipeline.py defines PipelineStageResult with fields: stage_num, stage_name, success, duration, exit_code, error_message, exception_type
pipeline_reporter.py defines StageResult(_CheckpointStageResult) adding output_files, errors, warnings
pipeline_summary.py uses PipelineStageResult while pipeline_reporter.py uses its own StageResult subclass
findings.suggestion: Consolidate into a single canonical StageResult in infrastructure/core/checkpoint.py (or a dedicated types module) with all fields present from the start; have pipeline.py and pipeline_reporter.py import and use it.

findings.summary: Module docstring advertises two top-level functions that do not exist at module scope
evidence: Line 5-6 lists 'get_performance_warnings' as a sub-domain 1 export — it is a method on StagePerformanceTracker, not a module-level function
Line 9 lists 'get_code_profiler' as a sub-domain 2 export — no such function exists anywhere in the file; the actual function is get_performance_monitor()
findings.suggestion: Remove 'get_performance_warnings' from the module-level export list or add a thin wrapper function. Rename 'get_code_profiler' to 'get_performance_monitor' in the docstring.

findings.summary: generate_pipeline_report has 8 parameters, 6 of which are Optional — a param bag that leaks report structure into the call site
evidence: def generate_pipeline_report(stage_results, total_duration, repo_root, test_results=None, validation_results=None, performance_metrics=None, error_summary=None, output_statistics=None, project_name=None)
6 of 9 parameters are Optional; callers must know which optional slots to fill
findings.suggestion: Introduce a ReportInput dataclass or builder to group the optional fields, so the function signature becomes generate_pipeline_report(stage_results, total_duration, repo_root, extras: ReportInput).

findings.summary: RetryableOperation.succeed() raises StopIteration to break a for-loop, violating PEP 479 and creating surprising control flow
evidence: Line 230: raise StopIteration  # Exit the retry loop inside succeed()
PEP 479 (active since Python 3.7) converts StopIteration raised inside a generator into RuntimeError; this pattern is fragile when used inside a generator or async context
findings.suggestion: Use a sentinel flag (e.g., self._done = True) and check it in __next__ to stop iteration, rather than raising StopIteration from succeed().

findings.summary: Three sections of generate_markdown_report emit literal placeholder strings instead of real content
evidence: Line 453: lines.append('Validation checks completed.')
Line 461: lines.append('Performance tracking completed.')
Line 471: lines.append('See error details in error summary report.')
findings.suggestion: Either render the actual data from validation_results, performance_metrics, and error_summary dictionaries, or remove the sections entirely if the data is not structured for display.

findings.summary: performance_context sets monitor.last_metrics via dynamic attribute assignment requiring a type: ignore comment
evidence: Line 170: monitor.last_metrics = metrics  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
PerformanceMonitor does not declare last_metrics as a class attribute, making the yielded object's post-context API invisible to type checkers and IDEs
findings.suggestion: Add last_metrics: Optional[PerformanceMetrics] = None to PerformanceMonitor.__init__, removing the need for dynamic assignment and the type: ignore.

findings.summary: _bytes_to_mb inner function is annotated Optional[int] but silently truncates sub-MB values to 0
evidence: Line 356-357: def _bytes_to_mb(b: Optional[int]) -> Optional[int]: return b // (1024 * 1024) if b is not None else None
Integer floor division means any memory usage under 1 MB reports as 0, which is misleading in a profiling context where fine-grained memory is relevant
findings.suggestion: Return float and use regular division: return b / (1024 * 1024) if b is not None else None, and update the annotation to Optional[float].
